title: triticum mexicanum
intro: Triticum mexicanum, also known as Mexican dwarf wheat, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Mexico. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high saponin content. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Triticum mexicanum can be prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
